Identity.Name: StingHTMWaterRiskZones
Identity.Definition: Water-safety risk zone classification per HTM 04-01. Drives augmented-care identification, dead-leg risk audit, daily-flush schedule generation, TMV3 mandatory placement, temperature-sensor requirements, and the Legionella + Pseudomonas risk register entries.

Governance.StandardsBasis: HTM 04-01:2016 (Safe water in healthcare premises); HSG 274 Legionnaires' disease; BS 8580-1:2019 (Risk assessment for Legionella)

Value.Definition: Augmented Care — patients at high risk of Pseudomonas / waterborne infection. Includes haematology, neonatal ICU, adult ICU, oncology, burns. Strictest HTM 04-01 controls; daily flush + outlet sampling.
Standard Clinical — general inpatient wards + outpatient clinical areas. HTM 04-01 standard controls; weekly flush of low-use outlets.
High Risk — renal dialysis, hydrotherapy pools, dental chair waterlines. Specialist water-quality requirements (dialysis: ultrapure water; hydrotherapy: pool chemistry; dental: BS 6700 + HTM 01-05).
Dental Chair — specific HTM 01-05 control regime for dental unit waterlines. Continuous disinfection + monthly sampling.
Low Use — outlets used < weekly. High Legionella risk due to stagnation. Requires increased flush frequency + monthly temperature monitoring.
Non-Clinical — offices, BOH, plant rooms. Standard BS 8580 risk assessment applies; relaxed flush requirements.
External — landscape irrigation, fire-fighting mains, external outlets. Outside HTM 04-01 scope but in the BS 8580 Legionella risk assessment.
